source: All-In Podcast — E274: SpaceX $2T case, Nvidia shock selloff
summary: AI fundamentals may be seasonal (summer slowdown), but the structural bull case remains intact alongside rising rates
summary_extended: Baker argues that AI usage and fundamental momentum may exhibit seasonal patterns, with college students using less ChatGPT and Claude during summer months and general productivity declining in warmer weather, creating a potential near-term headwind for AI-exposed equities. He frames this as a risk to watch but not a thesis-breaker, holding that three things can simultaneously be true: rising interest rates are a concern, AI fundamentals remain structurally strong (citing Anthropic as profitable and growing faster than any company in history), and the US is relatively advantaged amid geopolitical disruption. The investment implication is that any summer softness in AI usage metrics or revenue growth should be interpreted as cyclical noise rather than structural deterioration, and the broader bull case for AI infrastructure and application companies remains intact. Baker does not cite specific numbers or price targets but uses Anthropic's profitability milestone as evidence that AI monetization is real and accelerating.
detail: Baker notes AI usage has historically been seasonal (college students use less ChatGPT/Claude in summer, people work less when weather is nice). He flags this as a near-term risk to AI fundamental momentum but holds that three things can simultaneously be true: rates going up is concerning, AI fundamentals are getting stronger (Anthropic profitable, growing faster than any company in history), and America is relatively advantaged by geopolitical disruption.
confidence_evidence: Baker frames the seasonality as an open question ('we will see') but the broader bull case is stated with conviction
quote_fragment: AI fundamentals also appear a little seasonal
